nation: Chippewas of Kettle and Stony Point First Nation
auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
economic_notes: Southwind Corporate Development Inc. owns Points Preference Supermarket Inc., Kettle and Stony Point Gas & Convenience Inc., 9236058 Canada Inc., and Three Fires Group Inc. Indian Hills of Ipperwash Limited operates a golf course. Stony Point FSA Trust Fund holds $96,850,826. First Nation negotiating 1928 Surrender at Stony Point specific land claim.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Prior year (2021) comparative figures were restated due to a cash account correction of $466,852. Prior year included a $17,214,909 one-time settlement (Clench defalcation specific claim) which inflated 2021 revenue significantly. Two GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis: Southwind Corporate Development Inc. and Indian Hills of Ipperwash Limited. Three fully consolidated entities: Points Eagle Radio Inc., Chippewas of Kettle and Stony Point Settlement Trust (2016), and Chippewas of Kettle and Stony Point Clench Minors Trust (2021). Large portfolio investments of $122.4M held primarily in Stony Point FSA Trust Fund ($96.9M) and investment income of $8.99M reflects trust/investment returns. Auditor firm name not explicitly stated beyond 'Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants' based in London, Ontario. Net financial assets per statement of change is $136,920,411 for 2022 actual. Annual debt service reflects repayment only; $60,000 new draw on line of credit occurred. Expenses_infrastructure mapped to Public Works schedule.
